reforms.description: Denmark has implemented substantial public subsidies to support the wind turbine sector, which includes production subsidies, tax subsidies for co-operatively-owned wind turbines, and guaranteed prices for wind-generated electricity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The subsidies are designed to promote renewable energy production, which can lead to long-term environmental benefits and economic growth through the development of a competitive wind energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The subsidies have been in place since 1992 and have significantly supported the growth of the wind energy sector.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major commitment to renewable energy, significantly impacting the energy sector and contributing to Denmark's leadership in wind energy technology.
reforms.source_quote: The high cost of producing wind turbine electricity has required substantial public subsidies to enable the sector to expand, totalling almost DKr4 billion over the past six years.
